title: Episode 16 title question
wikitext:
[]: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by zlink64 » Fri Feb 05, 2016 5:46 am So I've got a question. The title of episode is "sickness onto death and..." and I never gave a second thought to/noticed the "and..." before. Does any one know what the "and..." is in reference to? My initial thought is that maybe it suppose to be like away of combing titles for the episode like "Sickness onto death and...splitting of the breast." But that is just me guessing and I'm not really familiar with what splitting of the breast even means. If anyone has a trust worthy link explaining the term splitting of the breast means or think they can explain it well I'd appreciate if shared.

[]: Re: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Feb 05, 2016 8:25 am ^ I guess it's meant to be ambiguous. The original Japanese has a soshite (そして), wich carries the connotation of "and then..." So it could be just a literal description of what's happening -> Shinji's (perceived) death, and then... I also checked my Platinum Booklet, but they didn't offer any insight on the soshite issue. They do, though explain the Episode title. The Sickness until Death part is directly lifted from the title of the most important work of Danish philosopher Aabye Kierkegaard (1813 - 1855). He sees in the existential desperation (wich he calls sickness until death) a fundamental determination of the human Dasein, which can only escape the Self, if one accepts Faith. Needless to say it's kind of a dated text, and certainly esoteric in today's standards. Splitting of the Breast, stems from the environment of Psychoanalysis. It describes a process in which an infant projects all the pleasant experiences onto one breast, while unpleasant gets attributed to the other. Sounds also quite dubious to me (by today's standards). So basically it's just another case of Anno reading about a cool and complicated sounding idea, and slaps it onto Episode that would go remotely well with those titles.

[]: Re: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by zlink64 » Fri Feb 05, 2016 10:06 am Thanks for the info. The thing is the sickness title doesn't just seem slapped on to me because the dialogue in that episode during Shinji being stuck inside the angel is basically repitition of kiekaggards ideas. And the the focus on Shinji not being able to swim in that episode is highly remincent of example in the book about A guy who can't swim. So I don't wanna assume the second part of the title is arbitrary in getting the episode when the first part is highly relevant.

[]: Re: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Feb 06, 2016 10:52 pm robersora wrote: Splitting of the Breast, stems from the environment of Psychoanalysis. It describes a process in which an infant projects all the pleasant experiences onto one breast, while unpleasant gets attributed to the other.

[]: Re: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by zlink64 » Sat Feb 06, 2016 11:07 pm I would love you forever if you could recommend a source/link. I've been thinking I'm just gonna search some random professor on the internet and email them for sources/answer or maybe just check some kind of ivy league psychology wiki assuming they have one...I know they have stuff like that for certain subjects. Thanks for the input btw. The way you word it just sound interesting because of the dualism. If I get response from an academic type I'll post here just to share.

[]: Re: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Feb 07, 2016 3:10 pm Monk Ed wrote: Never heard this before. The way I read it last, the concept was described as how, as one grows up, there comes a point when the breast changes from an object of desire to one of both desire and shame.

[]: Re: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by zlink64 » Sun Feb 07, 2016 10:58 pm If I do find something I'll give priority to answers that best match the version found in the booklet, since, that would make the most sense, unless it becomes obvious that tehe booklet just gave a shitty explanation.

[]: Re: Episode 16 title question             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Feb 08, 2016 5:29 am ^ I think (not sure), the content of the booklets was directly translated from the Japanese version. So if there's not a big localization error, bad research on the behalf of Japanese or bad research done by Anno, we're safe. (lol)
